_source: docs/THANGSENG_RULES_LOOKUP.md, 'Confirmed root raka table' (Rule 1 section). Verbatim transcription by Claude B, 2026-07-09, for build-time validation tooling (BACKLOG-006). Not new linguistic content - Claude A should confirm this transcription is accurate before it's treated as fully authoritative; flag any discrepancy rather than editing this file directly.
_schema: root: the confirmed root string as it appears in Garo forms (including its trailing raka mark if raka=true). has_raka: whether this root carries raka (·). notes: verbatim from the source table where present.

roots.notes: cha·a, cha·aha, cha·enga, cha·jok, cha·bo, cha·ja, cha·gen, cha·ode, cha·china
love sense: ka·saa, ka·saaha, ka·saenga, ka·saagen
kata, kataha, katenga, katbo, katgen - NEVER kat·anything

Ringa, ring·aha has raka but that's the NOUN ring's possessive, not this verb suffix - excluded from this table's scope. RC-CANDIDATE-009 (2026-07-23): corrections.json had "ring·na"/"ring·gen" wrongly using the raka'd ring·a="sing" homophone for "drink" - fixed to ringna/ringgen.

Tusia, tusienga, tus·aha, tusibo. RC-CANDIDATE-009 (2026-07-23): corrections.json "tusi·gen" (i will sleep) was a transcription error contradicted by RULE-023 (-gen never carries raka) - fixed to tusigen.

Agana, aganaha, aganenga, aganbo. RC-CANDIDATE-009 (2026-07-23): corrections.json had 3 question-form entries ("Agan·aha/jok/enga ma?") wrongly raka'd, contradicted by "Bia aganaha"/"Maiko aganenga?" in the same file - fixed to Aganaha/Aganjok/Aganenga.

Nama, namja, namgija - NEVER nam·a. STILL OPEN (NV-037, 2026-07-23): nam·e (corrections.json) and Nama·gipa (master_dictionary.json #874, kind/gentle) both show raka with derivational suffixes - not resolved by repo evidence, needs native check.
